behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is OS filesystem event delivery for nested paths (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). In-process fakes can emit idealized recursive events and miss watcher/backend limitations. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only filesystem watching/processing, with no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temporary directory tree. Create `root/sub/a.txt` while daemon is live and assert one successful pipeline output in the output directory for that nested file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure can be caught at a single object/function boundary: same path + window comparison and suppression decision (MOCK-MISS says no required cross-module invariant is stated). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is specifically "deduplication logic" and does not require real OS/network/subprocess, AI output, or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Test the dedup component directly with an injected clock: first request for `/x/f.txt` accepted, second request within window rejected, third request after window accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS duplicate event patterns (REAL-INFRA). A mock watcher can be made clean or unrealistically deterministic, hiding real duplicates/coalescing behavior (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model dependency and no agentic chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple low-level events (e.g., temp-write+rename or append burst), and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS watcher lifecycle behavior across delete/recreate events; failures occur at real filesystem/watch backend boundaries (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). In-process stubs typically do not reproduce handle invalidation/rebind semantics. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML decision or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real watched directory, remove the directory, recreate it at same path, then place a new file and verify it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Although ordering logic can be unit-tested, this behavior’s real failure boundary includes timestamp acquisition/event timing from actual filesystem operations under concurrent arrival (REAL-INFRA). Mocked timestamps/events can always satisfy intended order and miss real timing/granularity issues (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or agentic sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files near-simultaneously in a real watched directory with controlled creation times, capture processing order from daemon outputs/logs, and assert order matches filesystem creation timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real write timing/flush semantics and watcher event timing from OS/filesystem (REAL-INFRA). Fakes often model an atomic write and hide partial-file pickup bugs (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher levels: behavior is filesystem/process timing only, with no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Begin a slow/chunked write to a real input file while daemon runs, verify daemon does not process partial content mid-write, then verify processing occurs after writer closes/completes.
